[[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to [[:en:Naruhito|Emperor Naruhito]] !! [[:en:Succession to the Japanese throne|Order of Succession]] !! [[:en:Regent|Position in Regency]] !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! Title |- | [[File:Prince Fumihito Brazil 2015 (1).jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]] | Fumihito | November 30, 1965 | {{年数|1965|11|30}} years | Brother (2nd son of [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Emeritus Akihito]]) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Prince Hisahito]] | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Masahito, Prince Hitachi|Prince Masahito]] | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| %D0%BA%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%B8 (%D0%AF%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%8F)]] [[zh:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%9E)]] [[fa:%D8%B4%DB%8C%D9%86%D9%86%D9%88]] [[ja:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B]] Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to [[:en:Naruhito|Emperor Naruhito]] !! [[:en:Succession to the Japanese throne|Order of Succession]] !! [[:en:Regent|Position in Regency]] !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Emeritus Akihito]]) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Prince Hisahito]] | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Masahito, Prince Hitachi|Prince Masahito]] | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Imperial titles]] [[Category:Japanese princes]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:親王]] [[ko:%EC%B9%9C%EC%99%95]] %D0%BA%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%B8 (%D0%AF%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%8F)]] [[zh:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%9E)]] [[fa:%D8%B4%DB%8C%D9%86%D9%86%D9%88]] [[ja:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B]] '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! revision.text:
[[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to [[:en:Naruhito|Emperor Naruhito]] !! [[:en:Succession to the Japanese throne|Order of Succession]] !! [[:en:Regent|Position in Regency]] !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! Title |- | [[File:Prince Fumihito Brazil 2015 (1).jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|Prince Fumihito]] | Fumihito | November 30, 1965 | {{年数|1965|11|30}} years | Brother (2nd son of [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Emeritus Akihito]]) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Prince Hisahito]] | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Masahito, Prince Hitachi|Prince Masahito]] | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to [[:en:Naruhito|Emperor Naruhito]] !! [[:en:Succession to the Japanese throne|Order of Succession]] !! upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in [[:en:East Asia|East Asia]]. Originating from the royal traditions of [[:en:China|China]] (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in [[:en:Japan|Japan]] and [[:en:Vietnam|Vietnam]]. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the [[:en:Han dynasty|Han dynasty]], it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the [[:en:Emperor|Emperor]]. After the [[:en:Cao Wei|Wei Dynasty]], the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, [[:en:English language|Prince]]) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is [[:en:Highness|Your Highness (Denka)]]. === Current Shinnō ===
:;[[:en:Imperial Household Law|Imperial House Law]] Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, [[:en:Grand empress dowager|The Grand Empress Dowager]], [[:en:Empress dowager|The Empress Dowager]], '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, [[:en:Princess consort|Ōhi]], and [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]] are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male [[:en:Legitimacy (family law)|legitimate children]] and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled [[:en:Queen regnant|Joō]]. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''[[:en:Crown prince|Kōtaishi]]''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to [[:en:Naruhito|Emperor Naruhito]] !! [[:en:Succession to the Japanese throne|Order of Succession]] !! [[:en:Regent|Position in Regency]] !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! [[:en:Akihito|Emperor Emeritus Akihito]]) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Prince Hisahito of Akishino|Prince Hisahito]] | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | [[:en:Masahito, Prince Hitachi|Prince Masahito]] | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of [[:en:Hirohito|Emperor Showa]]) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:親王]] revision.text:
[[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh_classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. Originating from the royal traditions of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|WD=Q881|lt=Vietnam}}. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the {{ill|Han dynasty|en|Han dynasty|WD=Q7209|lt=Han dynasty}}, it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor}}. After the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei Dynasty}}, the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, {{ill|English language|en|English language|WD=Q1860|lt=Prince}}) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}. === Current Shinnō ===
:;{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law}} Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Grand empress dowager|en|Grand empress dowager|WD=Q124650494|lt=The Grand Empress Dowager}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=The Empress Dowager}}, '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|WD=Q7406779|lt=Ōhi}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}} are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male {{ill|Legitimacy (family law)|en|Legitimacy (family law)|WD=Q378505|lt=legitimate children}} and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}}. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''{{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Kōtaishi}}''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to {{ill|Naruhito|en|Naruhito|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}} !! {{ill|Succession to the Japanese throne|en|Succession to the Japanese throne|WD=Q1207573|lt=Order of Succession}} !! {{ill|Regent|en|Regent|WD=Q477406|lt=Position in Regency}} !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! Title |- | [[File:Prince Fumihito Brazil 2015 (1).jpg|100px]] | {{ill|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|en|Fumihito, Crown Prince of Japan|WD=Q311174|lt=Prince Fumihito}} | Fumihito | November 30, 1965 | {{年数|1965|11|30}} years | Brother (2nd son of {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Emeritus Akihito}}) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Prince Hisahito}} | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | {{ill|Masahito, Prince Hitachi|en|Masahito, Prince Hitachi|WD=Q725680|lt=Prince Masahito}} | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:親王]] [[ko:%EC%B9%9C%EC%99%95]] %D0%BA%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%B8 (%D0%AF%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%8F)]] [[zh:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%9E)]] [[fa:%D8%B4%DB%8C%D9%86%D9%86%D9%88]] [[ja:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B]] [[fa:شیننو]] Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, {{ill|English language|en|English language|WD=Q1860|lt=Prince}}) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}. === Current Shinnō ===
:;{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law}} Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Grand empress dowager|en|Grand empress dowager|WD=Q124650494|lt=The Grand Empress Dowager}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=The Empress Dowager}}, '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|WD=Q7406779|lt=Ōhi}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}} are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male {{ill|Legitimacy (family law)|en|Legitimacy (family law)|WD=Q378505|lt=legitimate children}} and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}}. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''{{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Kōtaishi}}''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to {{ill|Naruhito|en|Naruhito|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}} !! {{ill|Succession to the Japanese throne|en|Succession to the Japanese throne|WD=Q1207573|lt=Order of Succession}} !! {{ill|Regent|en|Regent|WD=Q477406|lt=Position in Regency}} !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q903422]] Wikidata name: Imperial prince [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. Originating from the royal traditions of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|WD=Q881|lt=Vietnam}}. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}

After the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei Dynasty}}, the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, {{ill|English language|en|English language|WD=Q1860|lt=Prince}}) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}. === Current Shinnō ===
:;{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law}} Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Grand empress dowager|en|Grand empress dowager|WD=Q124650494|lt=The Grand Empress Dowager}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=The Empress Dowager}}, '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|WD=Q7406779|lt=Ōhi}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}} are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male {{ill|Legitimacy (family law)|en|Legitimacy (family law)|WD=Q378505|lt=legitimate children}} and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.

A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! revision.text:
[[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia}}. Originating from the royal traditions of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}} (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|WD=Q881|lt=Vietnam}}. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the {{ill|Han dynasty|en|Han dynasty|WD=Q7209|lt=Han dynasty}}, it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor}}. After the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei Dynasty}}, the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, {{ill|English language|en|English language|WD=Q1860|lt=Prince}}) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)}}. === Current Shinnō ===
:;{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law}} Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Grand empress dowager|en|Grand empress dowager|WD=Q124650494|lt=The Grand Empress Dowager}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=The Empress Dowager}}, '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|WD=Q7406779|lt=Ōhi}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}} are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male {{ill|Legitimacy (family law)|en|Legitimacy (family law)|WD=Q378505|lt=legitimate children}} and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=Wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō}}. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''{{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Kōtaishi}}''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !! Relation to {{ill|Naruhito|en|Naruhito|WD=Q217096|lt=Emperor Naruhito}} !! {{ill|Succession to the Japanese throne|en|Succession to the Japanese throne|WD=Q1207573|lt=Order of Succession}} !! {{ill|Regent|en|Regent|WD=Q477406|lt=Position in Regency}} !! {{ill|Generational Line|ja|世数|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Generational Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 親族|1=kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q171318}} !! | {{年数|1965|11|30}} years | Brother (2nd son of {{ill|Akihito|en|Akihito|WD=Q37979|lt=Emperor Emeritus Akihito}}) | 1st | 1st | First generation | Ayano-miya (礼宮) |- | [[File:Prince Hisahito of Akishino 20201130.jpg|100px]] | {{ill|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|en|Prince Hisahito of Akishino|WD=Q844333|lt=Prince Hisahito}} | Hisahito | September 6, 2006 | {{年数|2006|9|6}} years | Nephew (1st son of Prince Fumihito) | 2nd | 2nd | Second generation | |- | [[File:Prince Masahito cropped 2 Prince Masahito Prince Albert II Princess Hanako and Yukiya Amano 20100713.jpg|100px]] | {{ill|Masahito, Prince Hitachi|en|Masahito, Prince Hitachi|WD=Q725680|lt=Prince Masahito}} | Masahito | November 28, 1935 | {{年数|1935|11|28}} years | Uncle (2nd son of {{ill|Hirohito|en|Hirohito|WD=Q34479|lt=Emperor Showa}}) | 3rd | 3rd | First generation | Yoshino-miya (義宮) |} * '''Honors''': Shinnō are granted the {{ill|Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|ja|大勲位菊花大綬章|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Grand Cordon of the Chrysanthemum|1=Grand Cordon of the Supreme Order of the Chrysanthemum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361043}} upon reaching adulthood. * '''English''': The term Prince is used universally for both Shinnō and Ō. {{translated page|ja|親王|version=101975120|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:親王]] [[ko:%EC%B9%9C%EC%99%95]] [[uk:%D0%86%D0%BC%D0%BF%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%B0%D1%82%D0%BE%D1%80%D1%81%D1%8C%D0%BA%D0%B8%D0%B9 %D0%BF%D1%80%D0%B8%D0%BD%D1%86 %D0%AF%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%BD%D1%96%D1%97]] [[ru:%D0%9F%D1%80%D0%B8%D0%BD%D1%86 %D0%BA%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%B8 (%D0%AF%D0%BF%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B8%D1%8F)]] [[zh:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%9E)]] [[fa:%D8%B4%DB%8C%D9%86%D9%86%D9%88]] [[ja:%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B]] [[fa:شیننو]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] [[fa:شیننو]][[ja:親王]][[ko:친왕]][[mn:Чин ван]][[ru:Принц крови (Япония)]][[uk:Імператорський принц Японії]][[zh-classical:親王]][[zh:親王 (東亞)]] {{ill|Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} refers to a title bestowed upon legitimate male children of emperors or high-Ranking royalty in {{ill|East Asia|en|East Asia|WD=Q27231|lt=East Asia|1=East Asia}}. Originating from the royal traditions of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}} (post-[[:en:Western Jin|Jin Dynasty]]), it was later adopted in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} and {{ill|Vietnam|en|Vietnam|WD=Q881|lt=Vietnam|1=Vietnam}}. Following these precedents, this title has sometimes been applied to male relatives of monarchs outside the [[:en:Sinophone world|Sinographic cultural sphere]] or used as the translation of [[Prince (royal title)|prince]] in English. == Overview == {{Infobox Royal styles |royal name = Shinnō (親王) |image = [[File:Japan Kouzoku Flag 16ben.svg|100px]] |dipstyle = {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)|1=Highness}}
''His Imperial Highness the Prince'' |color = #FFFFFF |background = #BE0026 }} {{see also|List of male members of the Imperial House of Japan}} The term {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}} originally referred to a sovereign but, following the {{ill|Han dynasty|en|Han dynasty|WD=Q7209|lt=Han dynasty|1=Han dynasty}}, it came to signify a title given to high-Ranking individuals subordinate to the {{ill|Emperor|en|Emperor|WD=Q39018|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor}}. After the {{ill|Cao Wei|en|Cao Wei|WD=Q320930|lt=Wei Dynasty|1=Cao Wei}}, the designation of Ō extended to include imperial male relatives. Among these, the title Shinnō was reserved for those of particularly close kinship to the emperor, a practice that later spread to Japan and the Korean Empire. == Shinnō in the Japanese Imperial Household == '''Shinnō''' (親王, {{ill|English language|en|English language|WD=Q1860|lt=Prince|1=English language}}) is a Rank or title within the {{ill|Imperial Family of Japan|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family of Japan|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581068}}. It also refers to individuals granted the Rank of Shinnō. The honorific style for Shinnō is {{ill|Highness|en|Highness|WD=Q1623587|lt=Your Highness (Denka)|1=Highness}}. === Current Shinnō ===
:;{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|WD=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law|1=Imperial Household Law}} Article 5 ::{{ill|The Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Grand empress dowager|en|Grand empress dowager|WD=Q124650494|lt=The Grand Empress Dowager|1=Grand empress dowager}}, {{ill|Empress dowager|en|Empress dowager|WD=Q7409248|lt=The Empress Dowager|1=Empress dowager}}, '''Shinnō''', {{ill|The Princess of Shinnō|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Princess of Shinnō|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}, {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|WD=Q7406779|lt=Ōhi|1=Princess consort}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō|1=Queen regnant}} are defined as members of the Imperial Family. :;Article 6 ::Male {{ill|Legitimacy (family law)|en|Legitimacy (family law)|WD=Q378505|lt=legitimate children|1=Legitimacy (family law)}} and {{ill|patrilineal|ja|嫡男|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/patrilineal|1=eldest Di son|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10102290}} grandchildren of the emperor within the second degree of kinship shall be Shinnō, and females shall be Naishinnō. Descendants beyond the second degree shall be Ō (for males) or Joō (for females). :;Article 7 ::If an Ō ascends to the throne, his brothers and sisters are to be elevated to the Rank of Shinnō or Naishinnō.
Under the current law, the title of Shinnō is reserved for legitimate male descendants of the emperor within the second degree of kinship. The corresponding title for female relatives is '''{{ill|Naishinnō|ja|内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishinnō|1=Imperial princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8015665}}'''. The wives of Shinnō are known as '''{{ill|Shinnōhi|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnōhi|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5361359}}'''. Male descendants beyond the second degree are titled {{ill|Ō|ja|王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ō|1=wang|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12087706}}, while females are titled {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|WD=Q19643|lt=Joō|1=Queen regnant}}. Shinnō who are heirs to the throne are further categorized: 1. '''{{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Kōtaishi|1=Crown prince}}''': The emperor's legitimate first son and primary heir. 2. '''{{ill|Kōtaison|ja|皇太孫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaison|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇太子|1=Crown prince|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q207293}}''': The legitimate grandson of the emperor, designated as an heir. A prince's Rank can change under these conditions: 1. Becoming a legitimate child or grandchild of the emperor through ascension (Article 6). 2. If a brother ascends to the throne (Article 7). * '''Current Shinnō (3 members):''' {| class=wikitable style=font-size:90% ! Photo !! Name !! Reading !! Birthdate !! Age !!
